message:
Add live WebSocket RPC bench: capnwasm vs capnweb, 3 workloads

Three new files:

  web/server.mjs           Node WebSocket server. Same port hosts both
                           ws://HOST:8081/capnwasm (capnwasm RpcSession)
                           and ws://HOST:8081/capnweb (capnweb RpcSession),
                           each implementing the same surface:
                             echoU8 / echoText / echoBinary / getChild
                           Run with `npm run server`.

  web/rpc.html             Three-card layout, one card per workload.

  web/src/rpc/main.ts      Connects both protocols up front, probes the
                           server, gates the Run button on it. Workloads:
                             1. Burst    200 echoU8 in same tick
                                         (microtask batching)
                             2. Pipe     getChild() → echoU8() chained
                                         on unresolved answer
                             3. Blob     64 KB binary echo round-trip

Headless Chromium run against the server (median of 3 iters):

  workload      capnwasm     capnweb     ratio
  Burst         12.5 µs/c    80 µs/c     6.4× faster
  Pipeline      300 µs       400 µs      1.33×
  64 KB blob    1.10 ms      1.40 ms     1.27×  (128 KB vs 171 KB wire)

Capnwasm wins 3 of 3 — the burst win is the headline (microtask
batching vs per-call sends). The pipeline + blob wins are smaller
because localhost RTT is microseconds; the gap widens once any real
network is in play.

Server start is currently manual (`npm run server` in another tab) —
embedding it as a Vite middleware is a follow-up.

     "capnweb": "^0.7.0"

@@ -0,0 +1,120 @@ + + + + + + capnwasm — live RPC bench (capnwasm vs capnweb) + + + + +
+
+

capnwasm RPC bench

+

+ Same WebSocket server, two RPC sessions, three workloads. + Each side connects to its own endpoint — + /capnwasm uses real Cap'n Proto wire format with + promise pipelining and microtask batching; + /capnweb uses capnweb's JSON-shaped wire. + The numbers below are what the browser actually pays. +

+
+ ← Landing · + Fetch playground · + Honest comparison +
+
+ +
+ + Checking ws://localhost:8081… + help +
+ +
+ + + +
+ +
+
+

Burst — 200 concurrent echoU8 calls

+

+ Fire 200 RPC calls in the same tick, await all. capnwasm coalesces + them into one transport.send via microtask batching; + capnweb sends them individually. Per-call latency is what a real + burst-of-clicks workload pays. +

+ + + + + + + + + + + +
capnwasm——
capnweb——
+
+ +
+

Pipelining — getChild() → echoU8() chain

+

+ Issue both calls at once: the second targets the first's + unresolved answer. capnwasm sends both Calls before any Return + arrives (1 round-trip). capnweb does the same in JSON. + Per-chain latency. +

+ + + + + + + + + + + +
capnwasm——
capnweb——
+
+ +
+

Big binary — echoBinary(64 KB) round-trip

+

+ 64 KB of raw bytes there and back. capnwasm ships them as + binary on the wire; capnweb base64-encodes (+33% bytes both + directions). The bytes column shows the cost. +

+ + + + + + + + + + + +
capnwasm——
capnweb——
+
+
+ +
+
+ + + +
